gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: TWAP cardinality-1 shortfall enables sandwich attack on swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn
candidate.claimed_root_cause: getTorusQuoteForTitanX falls back to oldestObservation when pool cardinality=1, producing a manipulable short TWAP window; adjustedTorusAmount (amountOutMinimum) is derived from this manipulated quote
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker sandwiches swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn, causing protocol to receive fewer TORUS per TITAN_X than fair value; attacker captures the spread as net TITAN_X profit

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Sets secondsAgo=900 then caps it to oldestObservation; if cardinality=1 and last swap was recent, secondsAgo collapses to seconds, making TWAP trivially manipulable
expectedTorusAmount comes from the potentially-short TWAP; adjustedTorusAmount applies 10% slippage on top of the already-manipulated quote
amountOutMinimum is set to adjustedTorusAmount; if TWAP is depressed 50%, minimum becomes 45% of fair value, leaving a 55% sandwich window
Public nonReentrant entry point; any unprivileged caller can trigger the swap; nonReentrant does not prevent multi-transaction sandwich

attacker_path.actor: Any unprivileged EOA holding sufficient TORUS to move the pool price
attacker_inputs: Timing of their own TORUS->TITAN_X sell to depress TWAP
Calling swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn as the victim transaction trigger
Back-run TORUS sell after the protocol swap inflates spot price
call_sequence: 1. Attacker sells large TORUS for TITAN_X, depressing TORUS spot price in pool
2. Attacker waits 2-5 blocks for short TWAP (cardinality=1) to reflect depressed price
3. Attacker front-runs: buys TORUS with TITAN_X, pushing spot price back up
4. Attacker calls swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn; getTorusQuoteForTitanX returns low quote from short TWAP; amountOutMinimum is low; swap executes at inflated spot without reverting
5. Attacker back-runs: sells TORUS at inflated price, capturing net TITAN_X profit
state_before: Pool spot price at fair value
Protocol holds TITAN_X allocated for current burn interval
state_after: Protocol received fewer TORUS than fair value for its TITAN_X
Attacker net TITAN_X balance increased by sandwich spread

extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: Net TITAN_X profit from sandwich spread; magnitude proportional to interval allocation size and TWAP depression depth
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol receives fewer TORUS burned per TITAN_X spent; burn efficiency degraded; protocol TITAN_X value transferred to attacker
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Attacker is entitled only to the INCENTIVE_FEE for calling swapTitanXForTorusAndBurn; the sandwich profit is extracted from the protocol's swap value beyond that entitlement

setup: Fork mainnet at block 25530134 (live_context captured_at block)
Call pool.slot0() and assert observationCardinality; if >1 and oldest observation >900s, REJECT
Fund attacker with TORUS sufficient to move pool price by >=20%
transaction_sequence: attacker.sell(TORUS -> TITAN_X, largeAmount) to depress price
vm.roll(block.number + 3) to let short TWAP settle
attacker.buy(TITAN_X -> TORUS) to restore spot price (front-run)
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If observationCardinality > 1 and oldest observation >= 900 seconds, the full 15-minute TWAP is used and manipulation cost becomes prohibitive; finding must be REJECTED

rejection_reason: Not rejected. Code path confirmed at L1124-1140 and L821-840. Critical precondition — pool observationCardinality=1 — is absent from live_context.json and cannot be asserted without a fork probe. The finding is plausible and the code path is exact, but promotion to HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE requires the fork test to confirm cardinality=1 on the live pool.
